pattern: Sort arr2 and use binary search to efficiently check whether any element falls within distance d of each arr1 element.
hints: For each element x in arr1, you need to know if any element of arr2 lies in the range [x-d, x+d].
Sorting arr2 lets you use binary search to find the closest element and check the distance in O(log n).
Use bisect to find the insertion point of x-d in the sorted arr2; if the found element is within x+d, skip x.

edge_inputs.description: borderline distance exactly d—should be blocked

stress.description: arr1 and arr2 of length 500, d=50
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = min(size, 500)
    arr1 = [rng.randint(-1000, 1000) for _ in range(n)]
    arr2 = [rng.randint(-1000, 1000) for _ in range(n)]
    d = rng.randint(0, 100)
    return [arr1, arr2, d]
